top_signals.title: Gilts Fall as Burnham’s Fiscal Flexibility Comments Spook Market
top_signals.summary: Gilts extended their retreat as UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham said he will seek “any flexibility” in the government’s fiscal rules, stoking concerns his administration will add to the nation’s heavy debt load.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Iran Claims Two Oil Tankers Exploded in Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said on Monday that two oil tankers had exploded and been immobilised after attempting to transit what it described as an unsafe southern route through the Strait of Hormuz, alleging they had been encouraged by the U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: PRA and FCA propose new captive insurance regime to drive UK growth and competitiveness
top_signals.summary: Innovative new proposals aim to establish the UK as a centre for the fast-growing captive insurance market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Growth and regulation - speech by Andrew Bailey
top_signals.summary: Given at The Financial and Professional Services Dinner, Mansion House
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Hackers were inside South Korea's diplomat training system for 9 months
top_signals.summary: Unidentified hackers compromised an online education system used by South Korea's diplomatic academy, stealing personal information belonging to former and current employees of the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
